verdicts.note: All evidence sources confirm this is the Gladly API/platform.
The developer documentation is at developer.gladly.com and help at help.gladly.com, confirming gladly.com domain.
Evidence confirms Gladly is a customer service platform with conversation, agent, and helpdesk features.
Evidence confirms REST API, Basic auth with email + API token, broad documentation, and that API access requires existing paid account with admin-granted 'API User' permission.
All sources confirm HTTP Basic Auth using Gladly login email as username and API token as password via Authorization header over HTTPS.

help.gladly.com states 'API tokens can be accessed and created by users who at least have an API user role' and 'Administrator should create a new user with an API role' - confirming admin-approval is required.
Evidence confirms API access requires existing account with 'API User' permission granted by admin, tokens generated via Settings > API Tokens.
developer.gladly.com clearly documents a REST API with comprehensive endpoint listings.
Evidence shows extensive endpoints covering agents, conversations, customers, tasks, topics, teams, inboxes, exports, webhooks, reports, etc. - justifying 'broad' classification.
Evidence confirms comprehensive REST API, separate Lookup API, and webhook event system as stated.

The URL provided (developer.gladly.com/rest/index.html) is the API documentation, NOT an MCP server. The record claims 'yes' but provides API docs as evidence. No evidence of an actual MCP server exists in the provided sources.
This URL is the REST API documentation, not an MCP server. The flags mention 'Third-party MCP server (StackOne) exists' but no URL is provided in evidence.
Evidence confirms API is functional and documented, but requires paid account with admin permissions - supporting 'buildable-with-caveats'.
Evidence clearly states API access requires existing account and admin-granted 'API User' permission, no public self-serve signup.
All three evidence URLs are valid and support the claims made.
0.75 is reasonable given clear documentation but confusion about existing MCP status.

Evidence supports no public self-serve tier, enterprise-gated access. Third-party MCP mentioned in flags but not evidenced. Sandbox environment not directly confirmed but dlthub mentions sandbox usage.

revised.name: Gladly
revised.one_liner: Gladly offers a broad, well-documented REST API (Basic auth via email + API token) for customer service data, but API access requires an existing paid enterprise account with admin-granted permissions rather than open self-serve signup.

auth_methods.notes: HTTP Basic Auth using Gladly login email as username and generated API token as password, sent via Authorization header over HTTPS.

revised.self_serve_notes: No public free/self-serve developer tier for the core enterprise platform; API access requires an existing paid subscription/account with a user granted the 'API User' permission. Tokens are then self-generated via Settings > API Tokens. A separate SMB Shopify-app version offers a 30-day trial but is a different limited product.

api_surface.notes: Comprehensive REST API covering agents, conversations, customers, tasks, topics, teams, inboxes, exports, webhooks, reports, and a separate Lookup API. Also has webhook event system.

revised.main_blocker: Requires an existing paid Gladly subscription and admin-granted 'API User' permission to generate API tokens; no public self-serve signup for API access.

evidence.supports: REST API surface, endpoints, and Basic Auth via Authorization header
How to generate API tokens and required permissions
Details on auth (Basic auth with email/token), endpoint list, and sandbox usage

flags: No public self-serve free tier; enterprise/contract-gated access
Third-party MCP server (StackOne) mentioned but not verified with evidence
Sandbox environment referenced by dlthub at {organization}.gladly.qa for testing once account provisioned
